critical.mandate: Val performs full validation — completeness, clarity, semantics, dependencies, and factual verification
Append validation results to the story file
Read the story template from {project-root}/_gaia/lifecycle/templates/story-template.md for reference
Auto-save all validation results to Val memory sidecar — no user prompt required

step.title: Load Story
step.action: Use the provided story key directly — skip the ask prompt below.
step.ask: Which story to validate? (provide story key, e.g., E1-S2)
step.action: Find and read the story file matching {story_key}-*.md in {implementation_artifacts}/
Read the story template from {project-root}/_gaia/lifecycle/templates/story-template.md for reference
Read {memory_path}/validator-sidecar/ground-truth.md if it exists (for factual verification)

step.title: Val Full Validation
step.action:
Perform full validation on the story file:
(a) YAML Frontmatter — verify all 14 required fields present and valid: key (pattern E{n}-S{n}), title (not empty), epic (valid reference), status (valid state), priority (P0/P1/P2), size (S/M/L/XL), points (matches sizing_map: S→2, M→5, L→8, XL→13), risk (high/medium/low), sprint_id (null or valid), depends_on (list), blocks (list), traces_to (list), date (valid date), author (not empty). FAIL if any required field is missing or invalid.
(b) Completeness — verify all template sections present: User Story, Acceptance Criteria, Tasks/Subtasks, Dev Notes, Technical Notes, Dependencies, Test Scenarios, Project Structure Notes, References, Dev Agent Record, Estimate, Definition of Done.
(c) Clarity — check each AC is testable and in Given/When/Then format, subtasks are actionable and linked to AC numbers, Definition of Done items are specific and measurable.
(d) Semantic Quality — duplicate detection: load all story files for the same epic, flag stories with >70% AC overlap as potential duplicates. ADR cross-reference: verify each referenced ADR exists in architecture.md with Accepted status. Flag Proposed/Deprecated references as warnings.
(e) Dependencies — verify all declared dependencies exist and resolve.
(f) Factual Verification — extract verifiable claims from the story (file paths, component references, API endpoints, dependency versions). Verify against filesystem and ground truth (if available). Classify findings as CRITICAL (broken reference), WARNING (outdated), INFO (style).
(g) Origin Field Validation (optional fields — backward compatible): The origin and origin_ref fields are OPTIONAL — stories without these fields are valid (backward compatibility). Missing origin/origin_ref fields do NOT cause errors and are accepted without warnings. If the origin field IS present, validate: - origin must be one of: "manual", "problem-solving", "triage", "add-feature", "sprint-planning", or null. An invalid origin enum value is a CRITICAL finding. - If origin is non-null, origin_ref must be non-empty (not null, not empty string). A non-null origin with empty or null origin_ref is a WARNING finding. If origin is null or absent, origin_ref is not validated (orphaned refs are acceptable).

check.action: In YOLO mode: SM auto-fixes all CRITICAL and WARNING findings without presenting to user. INFO findings are auto-acknowledged. Skip all user prompts in the fix loop.
Present findings table to user (severity, section, claim, finding, evidence).
SM (Nate) addresses each finding: - For each CRITICAL or WARNING finding: fix the story content directly - For each INFO finding: fix or acknowledge with a note
Present the corrected story sections to user for confirmation.
After SM applies all fixes, write the updated story file. Increment validation_attempt.

Re-run Val validation on the updated story (same checks as Step 2). - If zero findings: invoke status-sync with new_status='ready-for-dev'. Exit loop. Proceed to Step 4. - If findings remain AND validation_attempt < 3: SM fixes again, Val re-validates. - If findings remain AND validation_attempt = 3 (hard limit): keep status as 'validating', proceed to Step 4.

step.title: Save and Report
step.action: Append validation result at the END of the story file, after a horizontal rule (---) separator. Include: date, overall PASS/FAIL verdict, validation attempts count, and per-section findings.

Create a separate validation report file at {implementation_artifacts}/story-validation-{story_key}.md with YAML frontmatter: --- story_key: "{story_key}" validation_status: "PASS" or "FAIL" validated_at: "{ISO-8601 timestamp}" validation_attempts: {N} findings: - section: "{section name}" severity: "CRITICAL" or "WARNING" or "INFO" result: "PASS" or "FIXED" or "FAIL" issue: "{description if FAIL or FIXED}" --- Then detailed findings for each section.
step.template-output: Save the story file with status updated and validation report appended.

step.title: Save to Val Memory

next-step.on_pass: Create more stories, or run /gaia-sprint-plan when all stories are ready

alternative.context: When all stories are created and validated (ready-for-dev)
